facts.shortSummary: Kaleho dines with Trok in Hamri when Orden begs them to help find his missing daughter Hiyasa.
facts.longSummary: On the beach at Hamri, Kaleho joins Trok for an evening meal and the group recalls the prior tower adventure, including the older elemental mysteries tied to Airion the Mistspeaker. Orden then arrives in distress and explains that his daughter Hiyasa recently returned after a decade away, spoke of ancient treasure upriver, and vanished eight days ago. Trok and Kaleho agree to look into it and decide to seek out Ryu the next morning.

location.context: Trok's shack on the beach below the towers
location.notes: The opening scene takes place at Trok's home on the shore.

npcs.name: Orden
npcs.notes: A beach-dweller in Hamri who asks the party to find Hiyasa.

npcs.name: Hiyasa
npcs.notes: Recently returned to Hamri after ten years away and then vanished while seeking treasure upriver.

npcs.name: Airion the Mistspeaker
npcs.notes: Referenced in the recap of the previous tower adventure.

facts.shortSummary: The next morning the party recruits Ryu and Nerissa, then questions Abelard at the Leviathan before leaving Hamri.
facts.longSummary: At breakfast the next morning, Trok and Kaleho bring Orden's problem to Ryu and explain the plan to search upriver. Nerissa, an older scholar living nearby, introduces herself over tea and biscuits and volunteers to join once she hears about the possibility of ancient ruins. The group then stops at the Leviathan, where Abelard confirms that a woman matching Hiyasa's description went up the Mirmir after boasting of treasure in an ancient tomb.

location.context: Ryu's tower, the Leviathan Inn, and final preparations in town
location.notes: The beat combines the morning recruitment and rumor-gathering scenes before the party leaves Hamri.

npcs.name: Abelard
npcs.notes: Confirms that Hiyasa headed upriver after speaking of an ancient tomb.

npcs.name: Hiyasa
npcs.notes: Abelard recognizes her description and route.

facts.shortSummary: Ryu leads the party up the Mirmir and through the forest to Yerkir-khor's buried monument.
facts.longSummary: Leaving Hamri, the party climbs above the waterfall and follows the Mirmir inland through autumn forest, relying on Ryu's tracking and Blu's scouting to stay on Hiyasa's trail. They make camp after dark, resume the search the next day, find Hiyasa's abandoned campsite, and follow the cut trail farther from the river. After a day and a half of pursuit, they reach a massive stone flower-like monument inscribed in Terran as the resting place of Yerkir-khor the Undaunted, inspect the ropes and open trap door, and stop just before anyone begins the descent.

location.context: tracking Hiyasa upriver and through the inland forest
location.notes: The beat spans departure, overnight travel, the abandoned campsite, and surface inspection of the monument.

npcs.name: Hiyasa
npcs.notes: The party follows the signs of her recent passage.

npcs.name: Blu
npcs.notes: Scouts during the forest search.

npcs.name: Yerkir-khor
npcs.notes: Named in the Terran inscription on the monument.

items.name: Terran monument inscription
items.notes: Identifies Yerkir-khor as the Undaunted Prince of Earth, Lord of the Rock Below, and Guardian of the Crystal Skies.

facts.shortSummary: The party descends into the tomb, survives the first traps, and enters the crystal-mosaic halls.
facts.longSummary: Trok starts down the shaft, discovers carved handholds and a mosaic floor far below, and the party brings Nerissa's floating lights to see the crystal-cavern design. The descent goes wrong when a hidden blade trap knocks Trok down and Kaleho falls through the shaft, leaving the group split between the top and the hall below. After regrouping with ropes and caution, they study the glittering mosaic chamber, recognize more of the tomb's strange stonework, and reach the statue chamber just before the carved guardians fully become a fight.

location.context: entrance shaft, crystal mosaic hall, and upper statue chamber
location.notes: The party has fully entered the buried complex.

npcs.name: Blu
npcs.notes: Remains with Ryu while the others begin the descent.

npcs.name: Yerkir-khor
npcs.notes: The party continues interpreting the tomb in light of the monument inscription.

items.name: crystal-cavern mosaic
items.notes: A multicolored floor mosaic below the shaft depicts glittering caves or gemstone caverns.

items.name: blade trap
items.notes: A hidden blade swings through the shaft during the descent.

facts.shortSummary: The tomb's guardians awaken, forcing the party through a hard fight against stone gargoyles and a serpent statue.
facts.longSummary: The carved guardians animate into a fight with several winged stone gargoyles and a serpent-headed statue construct. Trok grows and smashes the small stone creatures, Nerissa's thunderous magic cracks their bodies, Kaleho shoots from range, and Ryu and Blu dive in to stop gargoyles that try to reach and activate the larger statue. When the last guardian shatters, the party leaves initiative and begins inspecting the broken defenders and the statue they had been trying to manipulate.

location.context: guardian chamber near the upper halls
location.notes: This chamber houses the first active defenders of the tomb.

npcs.name: Blu
npcs.notes: Dive-bombs gargoyles during the fight.

items.name: loose amethyst gem
items.notes: Set in a gold band on the serpent statue and apparently targeted by the gargoyles.

mainEnemies.name: stone gargoyles
mainEnemies.notes: Winged stone guardians animated to defend the chamber.

mainEnemies.name: serpent statue construct
mainEnemies.notes: A snake-headed stone figure tied to the chamber's defenses.

combat.notes: The fight is contained in this beat and ends when the last active stone guardian is destroyed.

facts.shortSummary: After the first fight, the party follows Hiyasa's tracks through locked doors, sticky mud, and lightning crystals toward the inner tomb.
facts.longSummary: With the first guardians destroyed, the party finds stone keys on the shattered gargoyles and confirms that Hiyasa's trail leads through a locked door. They heal, mark hazards, and descend through mirror-image rooms and corridors while trying to preserve the tracks. The lower route forces them to solve or bypass a sticky mud-like floor, then crawl beneath a lightning-crystal trap around a Terran warning to the faithful of Yerkir-khor, bringing them to the chamber where Hiyasa is trapped.

location.context: lower corridors, locked rooms, mud trap, and lightning-crystal stair
location.notes: The group advances steadily from the first guardian room toward the inner sanctum.

npcs.name: Hiyasa
npcs.notes: Her trail shows where she went after surviving earlier traps.

npcs.name: Yerkir-khor
npcs.notes: Named in the warning that threatens the unfaithful with entombment.

items.name: stone keys
items.notes: Found around the broken gargoyles' necks and used to open the locked route.

items.name: lightning-crystal trap
items.notes: Fires from the railing crystals unless the party crawls beneath it.

facts.shortSummary: The party finds Hiyasa trapped in stone beside the tomb's great guardian and fails to satisfy its demand for tribute.
facts.longSummary: In the inner chamber the party finally finds Hiyasa alive, dehydrated, and encased in stone from the upper thighs down. The room is covered in gem mosaics showing a stone-and-earth lord shaping valleys, flattening mountains, receiving offerings, and being honored by humans and dwarves. A large statue steps down from the tomb altar and demands tribute for Yerkir-khor, but the party's attempts to interpret the chamber and prepare offerings only bring them closer to confrontation.

location.context: inner sanctum before the central altar
location.notes: The final chamber contains Hiyasa, tribute jars, murals, and the tomb's greatest guardian.

npcs.name: Hiyasa
npcs.notes: Alive but trapped in stone when the party finds her.

npcs.name: Yerkir-khor
npcs.notes: The chamber's inscriptions and guardian speak in his name.

items.name: gem-stoppered jars
items.notes: Six large jars stand behind the altar, each topped with a different gemstone.

items.name: inner sanctum mosaics
items.notes: Depict Yerkir-khor shaping earth, receiving offerings, and ruling over scenes of farming, mining, and trade.

facts.shortSummary: When tribute fails, the guardian attacks and the party scrambles to survive the opening phase of the tomb battle.
facts.longSummary: The party offers shells, a pearl, and Kaleho's treasured merfolk book, but the guardian rejects each ocean-linked tribute as an insult to Yerkir-khor and grows angrier. Ryu throws a spear to start the fight, Blu enters the blue-lit room, and initiative begins. Kaleho opens from hiding, Nerissa uses sound magic and inspiration, Trok activates Eldritch Maul, Ryu and Blu strike hard, and Hiyasa warns that the guardian can turn stone to mud and back again.

location.context: inner sanctum around Hiyasa's stone prison
location.notes: Combat begins in the same room where tribute negotiations failed.

npcs.name: Hiyasa
npcs.notes: Still trapped in stone while the fight begins around her.

npcs.name: Blu
npcs.notes: Streaks down to attack the guardian during the opening rounds.

npcs.name: Yerkir-khor
npcs.notes: The guardian frames the rejected offerings as insults to Yerkir-khor.

items.name: ocean offerings
items.notes: Shells, a pearl, and a merfolk book are presented as tribute and rejected.

mainEnemies.name: stone guardian of Yerkir-khor
mainEnemies.notes: The enormous construct that judges tribute in the inner tomb.

combat.notes: The opening rounds establish the guardian's earth-shaping powers and hostility to ocean-linked tribute.

facts.shortSummary: The party begins to turn the fight by hammering the guardian with thunder, psychic force, weapons, and Blu's attacks.
facts.longSummary: As the battle grinds on, Nerissa moves away and keeps using piercing sound while the others try to keep pressure on the stone colossus. The guardian fails to laugh at Tasha's Hideous Laughter, retreats and reshapes stone around the chamber, and keeps forcing the party to reposition. Trok tries to close with Eldritch Maul and a water-tattoo charm, Ryu and Blu continue striking from range and above, and Kaleho looks for a way to keep sneak attack pressure on the guardian before the floor becomes the next crisis.

location.context: inner sanctum during the running fight with the guardian
location.notes: The party is still engaged in continuous combat with the same foe.

npcs.name: Hiyasa
npcs.notes: Still stuck in stone while the party tries to win quickly.

npcs.name: Blu
npcs.notes: Continues dive-bombing the guardian during the middle phase.

mainEnemies.name: stone guardian of Yerkir-khor
mainEnemies.notes: Still the only major enemy in the final chamber.

combat.notes: This phase centers on sustained damage, failed control magic, and positional pressure before the mud crisis.

facts.shortSummary: The guardian turns the floor to mud and nearly entombs Trok, but Ryu kills it before the stone can close forever.
facts.longSummary: In the battle's most dangerous turn, the guardian liquefies the floor, restraining Nerissa and Hiyasa while a stone hand grabs Kaleho. It then knocks Trok unconscious, throws him into the mud, and leaves him sinking as the floor begins to harden back into stone. Ryu kills the guardian, revives Trok with a swarm of sardine spirits, and the party scrambles through the aftermath while Hiyasa frees herself, tries to pocket a jar, relaxes only after Trok invokes Orden, and begins sketching the map that led her to the tomb.

location.context: inner sanctum as the stone floor turns to mud
location.notes: The battlefield itself becomes the main hazard in the closing rounds.

npcs.name: Hiyasa
npcs.notes: Freed once the guardian is destroyed, then begins explaining what brought her to the tomb.

npcs.name: Orden
npcs.notes: Trok invokes Orden to convince Hiyasa the party came to rescue her.

items.name: gem-stoppered jars
items.notes: Hiyasa tries to slip one into her pack immediately after the fight.

items.name: Hiyasa's map sketch
items.notes: She begins drawing the map that marked three long-abandoned tombs.

mainEnemies.name: stone guardian of Yerkir-khor
mainEnemies.notes: Destroyed after nearly killing Trok by entombment.

combat.notes: Ryu's decisive attack ends the fight before the mud can harden around Trok.

facts.shortSummary: With Hiyasa safe, the party loots the tomb, returns to Hamri, and identifies the strangest treasure from the jars.
facts.longSummary: After the guardian falls, Hiyasa explains that she thought this ancient tomb would be safer to rob than ancestral Mawar graves and admits she brought scrolls and a map from her years away. The party opens the gem-stoppered jars, finding archaic coins, rough gemstones, a pearl, yellow dust protecting ancient papers, red liquid that proves to be spoiled wine, and an obsidian jar with a one-way void that later shatters. They leave the tomb, disguise the entrance, return to Hamri a few days later, learn that the coins are ancient Orban, identify the pearl as a pearl of power, split about 600 gold of value, give Hiyasa a finder's fee, and leave the fragile unknown papers for later study.

location.context: tomb aftermath, return travel, and division of spoils at the inn
location.notes: The beat spans the immediate aftermath in the tomb and the later return to town.

npcs.name: Hiyasa
npcs.notes: Returns with the party, accepts a finder's fee, and offers more information about the other map marks for payment.

npcs.name: Orden
npcs.notes: The party discusses whether to give money to him and Hiyasa.

items.name: Hiyasa's treasure map
items.notes: Shows three marked locations, including the tomb they just explored.

items.name: ancient Orban coins and gems
items.notes: Together worth about 600 gold after the return to Hamri.

items.name: pearl of power
items.notes: Recovered from the jars and eventually identified as a magic item that can restore a spell slot.

items.name: yellow dust and ancient papers
items.notes: The magical dust preserves a fragile sheaf of papers in an unknown language.

items.name: obsidian void jar
items.notes: Appears to contain a hole to nowhere but shatters after removal from the tomb.
